content: We can frame the original task-agnostic Self-Refine framework as a base layer that can be augmented with multiple inconsistent perspective sampling from modern LLM self-reflection mechanisms, creating a two-stage hierarchical self-refinement system that retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervision. This hybrid system addresses a key unmet need of original Self-Refine: it avoids the common failure mode where single-perspective self-feedback repeats the same blind spots the LLM made in its initial output.
